categories.name: أَجْزَاءُ الرَّأْسِ وَالْعُنُقِ
categories.nameEn: Parts of the head and neck

items.verseEn: We will brand him upon the snout.

items.verse: قُلْ ءَامِنُوا۟ بِهِۦٓ أَوْ لَا تُؤْمِنُوٓا۟ ۚ إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ أُوتُوا۟ ٱلْعِلْمَ مِن قَبْلِهِۦٓ إِذَا يُتْلَىٰ عَلَيْهِمْ يَخِرُّونَ لِلْأَذْقَانِ سُجَّدًۭا
items.verseEn: Say, "Believe in it or do not believe." Indeed, those who were given knowledge before it - when it is recited to them, they fall upon their faces in prostration,

items.verse: إِذْ جَآءُوكُم مِّن فَوْقِكُمْ وَمِنْ أَسْفَلَ مِنكُمْ وَإِذْ زَاغَتِ ٱلْأَبْصَـٰرُ وَبَلَغَتِ ٱلْقُلُوبُ ٱلْحَنَاجِرَ وَتَظُنُّونَ بِٱللَّهِ ٱلظُّنُونَا۠
items.verseEn: [Remember] when they came at you from above you and from below you, and when eyes shifted [in fear], and hearts reached the throats, and you assumed about Allāh [various] assumptions.
Then why, when it [i.e., the soul at death] reaches the throat

categories.name: أَنْوَاعُ الْمَسَالِكِ وَالْفَتَحَاتِ
categories.nameEn: Words for types of paths or openings

items.verse: فَلَمَّا بَلَغَا مَجْمَعَ بَيْنِهِمَا نَسِيَا حُوتَهُمَا فَٱتَّخَذَ سَبِيلَهُۥ فِى ٱلْبَحْرِ سَرَبًۭا
items.verseEn: But when they reached the junction between them, they forgot their fish, and it took its course into the sea, slipping away.

items.verse: وَإِن كَانَ كَبُرَ عَلَيْكَ إِعْرَاضُهُمْ فَإِنِ ٱسْتَطَعْتَ أَن تَبْتَغِىَ نَفَقًۭا فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ أَوْ سُلَّمًۭا فِى ٱلسَّمَآءِ فَتَأْتِيَهُم بِـَٔايَةٍۢ ۚ وَلَوْ شَآءَ ٱللَّهُ لَجَمَعَهُمْ عَلَى ٱلْهُدَىٰ ۚ فَلَا تَكُونَنَّ مِنَ ٱلْجَـٰهِلِينَ
items.verseEn: And if their evasion is difficult for you, then if you are able to seek a tunnel into the earth or a stairway into the sky to bring them a sign, [then do so]. But if Allāh had willed, He would have united them upon guidance. So never be of the ignorant.

items.verse: أَلَمْ تَرَ أَنَّ ٱللَّهَ أَنزَلَ مِنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ مَآءًۭ فَأَخْرَجْنَا بِهِۦ ثَمَرَٰتٍۢ مُّخْتَلِفًا أَلْوَٰنُهَا ۚ وَمِنَ ٱلْجِبَالِ جُدَدٌۢ بِيضٌۭ وَحُمْرٌۭ مُّخْتَلِفٌ أَلْوَٰنُهَا وَغَرَابِيبُ سُودٌۭ
items.verseEn: Do you not see that Allāh sends down rain from the sky, and We produce thereby fruits of varying colors? And in the mountains are tracts, white and red of varying shades and [some] extremely black.

categories.name: أَلْفَاظٌ تَصِفُ اللَّيْلَ أَوْ فَتَرَاتِهِ الْاِنْتِقَالِيَّة
categories.nameEn: Words describing the night or its transitional periods

items.verse: أَقِمِ ٱلصَّلَوٰةَ لِدُلُوكِ ٱلشَّمْسِ إِلَىٰ غَسَقِ ٱلَّيْلِ وَقُرْءَانَ ٱلْفَجْرِ ۖ إِنَّ قُرْءَانَ ٱلْفَجْرِ كَانَ مَشْهُودًۭا
items.verseEn: Establish prayer at the decline of the sun [from its meridian] until the darkness of the night and [also] the Qur’ān [i.e., recitation] of dawn. Indeed, the recitation of dawn is ever witnessed.
So I swear by the twilight glow
And [by] the night when it covers with darkness,
And by the night as it closes in

categories.name: أَجْزَاءُ الْجَسَدِ فِي سِيَاقٍ مَجَازِيٍّ
categories.nameEn: Words for body parts used in metaphorical or idiomatic expressions

items.verse: فَتَوَلَّىٰ بِرُكْنِهِۦ وَقَالَ سَـٰحِرٌ أَوْ مَجْنُونٌۭ
items.verseEn: But he turned away with his supporters and said, "A magician or a madman."

items.verse: ۞ وَلَكُمْ نِصْفُ مَا تَرَكَ أَزْوَٰجُكُمْ إِن لَّمْ يَكُن لَّهُنَّ وَلَدٌۭ ۚ فَإِن كَانَ لَهُنَّ وَلَدٌۭ فَلَكُمُ ٱلرُّبُعُ مِمَّا تَرَكْنَ ۚ مِنۢ بَعْدِ وَصِيَّةٍۢ يُوصِينَ بِهَآ أَوْ دَيْنٍۢ ۚ وَلَهُنَّ ٱلرُّبُعُ مِمَّا تَرَكْتُمْ إِن لَّمْ يَكُن لَّكُمْ وَلَدٌۭ ۚ فَإِن كَانَ لَكُمْ وَلَدٌۭ فَلَهُنَّ ٱلثُّمُنُ مِمَّا تَرَكْتُم ۚ مِّنۢ بَعْدِ وَصِيَّةٍۢ تُوصُونَ بِهَآ أَوْ دَيْنٍۢ ۗ وَإِن كَانَ رَجُلٌۭ يُورَثُ كَلَـٰلَةً أَوِ ٱمْرَأَةٌۭ وَلَهُۥٓ أَخٌ أَوْ أُخْتٌۭ فَلِكُلِّ وَٰحِدٍۢ مِّنْهُمَا ٱلسُّدُسُ ۚ فَإِن كَانُوٓا۟ أَكْثَرَ مِن ذَٰلِكَ فَهُمْ شُرَكَآءُ فِى ٱلثُّلُثِ ۚ مِنۢ بَعْدِ وَصِيَّةٍۢ يُوصَىٰ بِهَآ أَوْ دَيْنٍ غَيْرَ مُضَآرٍّۢ ۚ وَصِيَّةًۭ مِّنَ ٱللَّهِ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ عَلِيمٌ حَلِيمٌۭ
items.verseEn: And for you is half of what your wives leave if they have no child. But if they have a child, for you is one fourth of what they leave, after any bequest they [may have] made or debt. And for them [i.e., the wives] is one fourth if you leave no child. But if you leave a child, then for them is an eighth of what you leave, after any bequest you [may have] made or debt. And if a man or woman leaves neither ascendants nor descendants but has a brother or a sister, then for each one of them is a sixth. But if they are more than two, they share a third, after any bequest which was made or debt, as long as there is no detriment [caused]. [This is] an ordinance from Allāh, and Allāh is Knowing and Forbearing.

items.verse: أَوْ كَصَيِّبٍۢ مِّنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ فِيهِ ظُلُمَـٰتٌۭ وَرَعْدٌۭ وَبَرْقٌۭ يَجْعَلُونَ أَصَـٰبِعَهُمْ فِىٓ ءَاذَانِهِم مِّنَ ٱلصَّوَٰعِقِ حَذَرَ ٱلْمَوْتِ ۚ وَٱللَّهُ مُحِيطٌۢ بِٱلْكَـٰفِرِينَ
items.verseEn: Or [it is] like a rainstorm from the sky within which is darkness, thunder and lightning. They put their fingers in their ears against the thunderclaps in dread of death. But Allāh is encompassing of the disbelievers.

items.verse: يَوْمَ يُكْشَفُ عَن سَاقٍۢ وَيُدْعَوْنَ إِلَى ٱلسُّجُودِ فَلَا يَسْتَطِيعُونَ
items.verseEn: The Day the shin will be uncovered and they are invited to prostration but they [i.e., the disbelievers] will not be able,

harf.hint: A metal of great might, mentioned as something people could be turned into, even if they wished to escape resurrection.

deduction.title: The Towering Challenge
deduction.intro: Before a decisive battle, a formidable figure steps forward, their immense presence casting a shadow of doubt over the opposing army. Confident in their own might, this individual issues a challenge for single combat, believing none would dare to face them. This moment would become a testament to the nature of true strength, proving that worldly power is nothing before the will of Allah.
clues: I was a champion of my people, known for my immense physical size and strength.
My army and I advanced against a small band of believers led by their king, Talut.
Full of arrogance, I strode out between the armies and demanded a duel to decide the battle's outcome.
I looked upon my opponents with contempt, certain that my power was unmatchable.
A young shepherd boy, armed with only a sling, bravely stepped forward to accept my challenge.
Despite my imposing stature and armor, I was defeated by a single projectile from this young challenger, who would later become a great prophet and king.

identity.label: Who Am I?
options: Jalut (Goliath)
Namrud
Fir'awn (Pharaoh)
The Samiri
Tubba'

trial.label: What Was My Challenge?
options: Single combat against a young believer
Building a tower to reach the heavens
Demanding prostration from a prophet
Fashioning a golden calf for worship
Debating with Prophet Ibrahim about Allah
trial.answer: Single combat against a young believer

location.label: What Was My Downfall?
options: A stone from a sling
A devastating flood
A swarm of birds with baked-clay stones
Being swallowed by the earth
Drowning in a parted sea

outcome.label: What Was the Result of My Defeat?
options: The victory of Talut's army and the rise of Dawud
My entire army being drowned in pursuit of a prophet
The establishment of the Kaaba as a house of worship
My people being punished with a mighty shriek
My kingdom being inherited by Prophet Sulayman
outcome.answer: The victory of Talut's army and the rise of Dawud

deduction.arabic: فَهَزَمُوهُم بِإِذْنِ ٱللَّهِ وَقَتَلَ دَاوُۥدُ جَالُوتَ وَءَاتَىٰهُ ٱللَّهُ ٱلْمُلْكَ وَٱلْحِكْمَةَ وَعَلَّمَهُۥ مِمَّا يَشَآءُ ۗ وَلَوْلَا دَفْعُ ٱللَّهِ ٱلنَّاسَ بَعْضَهُم بِبَعْضٍۢ لَّفَسَدَتِ ٱلْأَرْضُ وَلَـٰكِنَّ ٱللَّهَ ذُو فَضْلٍ عَلَى ٱلْعَـٰلَمِينَ
deduction.verse: So they defeated them by permission of Allāh, and David killed Goliath, and Allāh gave him the kingship and wisdom [i.e., prophethood] and taught him from that which He willed. And if it were not for Allāh checking [some] people by means of others, the earth would have been corrupted, but Allāh is the possessor of bounty for the worlds. (2:251)

scramble.hint: A prophet's plea for divine help when feeling overwhelmed by his people's rejection.
scramble.verseEn: So he invoked his Lord, "Indeed, I am overpowered, so help."
